# Build and publish the cohort website {#sec-website}

Each cohort gets its own website, generated from the template in `course-dev`. The website is the learners' home base: it holds the pre-course work, the schedule, and links to everything. This chapter is the checklist to generate it, fill in its details, render it, and publish it.

Replace `course` with your course slug and `001` with your iteration tag throughout.

## Step 1: Generate the site from the template

- [ ] Go to `course-dev/website` on GitHub.
- [ ] Click **Use this template**, then **Create a new repository**.
- [ ] Set the owner to `course-001` and the repository name to `website`.
- [ ] Create the repository.

This produces `course-001/website` as an independent repository with clean history and no live link back to the template. It is a frozen snapshot you can edit freely without affecting the template or other cohorts.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**New educator** — if the **Use this template** button is missing, the template flag is not set on `course-dev/website`. Go back to @sec-create-orgs, step 2, and check **Template repository** in its settings.
:::

## Step 2: Clone the new website locally

- [ ] In RStudio, create a new project from version control, pointing at `course-001/website`.
- [ ] Confirm the project opens and you can see the files, including `_quarto.yml` and `_variables.yml`.

## Step 3: Fill in the iteration variables

The template uses two configuration files. `_variables.yml` holds the values that change every cohort; `_quarto.yml` is the site configuration. Open `_variables.yml` and replace every placeholder with this cohort's real values.

Fields you will set in `_variables.yml`:

| Field | What to put |
|---|---|
| Course title | The human-readable course title |
| Course slug | Your `course` slug |
| Iteration | This cohort's tag (`001`) |
| Cohort label | A free-form label, for example "Spring 2026" |
| Start and end dates | ISO dates for the cohort |
| Timezone | The IANA timezone for live sessions |
| Course website URL | The published URL of this site |
| GitHub organization | `course-001` |
| Instructor name, ORCID, affiliation, GitHub, email | Your details |

::: {.callout-important}
Do not invent values you do not have. Leave a field as its placeholder, or as a clearly marked "to fill in," rather than guessing a URL, ORCID, or DOI. A placeholder is honest; a wrong value is a bug a learner will hit.
:::

- [ ] Replace each placeholder in `_variables.yml` with a real value or a clearly marked to-do.
- [ ] Save the file.

## Step 4: Update the site configuration

Open `_quarto.yml` and update the fields that are specific to this cohort:

| Field | What to put |
|---|---|
| Project / site title | Match the iteration |
| Site URL | The published URL of this cohort site |
| Repository URL | `https://github.com/course-001/website` |
| Repository branch | Usually `main` |

- [ ] Update each cohort-specific field in `_quarto.yml`.
- [ ] Leave template-level fields (theme, navbar, sidebar structure) alone. If you find yourself changing those every cohort, push the change back into `course-dev/website` so future cohorts inherit it.

## Step 5: Render the site locally

- [ ] In the RStudio terminal, run `quarto preview`.
- [ ] Confirm the site builds without errors and opens in your browser.
- [ ] Click through the pre-course work pages and confirm your variable values flow through (the title, dates, and instructor name should all show correctly).

If the variables show as placeholders in the rendered pages, you missed an entry in step 3.

## Step 6: Publish the site

- [ ] Commit your changes and push them to `course-001/website`.
- [ ] Enable GitHub Pages for the repository (Settings, then Pages), or publish with `quarto publish gh-pages` from the terminal.
- [ ] Confirm the published URL loads and matches the site URL you set in the configuration.

## Optional: web analytics

If you want to know how learners use the site, add privacy-friendly analytics with Plausible (<https://plausible.io>):

- [ ] Register the cohort site's domain on plausible.io.
- [ ] In Plausible, open **Site settings**, then **Tracker**, and copy the generated script tag.
- [ ] Save it as `plausible.html` next to `_quarto.yml`.
- [ ] Reference it from `_quarto.yml` under the HTML format's include-in-header setting.
- [ ] Re-render and confirm visits appear in the Plausible dashboard.

This is optional. The workshop runs fine without it.

## Verify as the test-learner

- [ ] Open the published site in your test-learner browser (logged out of the teacher account).
- [ ] Confirm the pre-course work pages are reachable and the links work.

A site that a logged-out learner can read end to end is ready to hand out.
